body.title: watch_problems tests leak data to real instance schema
body.result: test_data=true stamp in add_watch_problem() when IGOR_TEST_MODE=1; DELETE cleanup added to conftest _test_data_lifecycle teardown
body.description:
test_watch_problems.py has accumulated test data in the real instance.watch_problems table (~26 rows each for 'some condition', 'ticket description scope affected files', etc.). The pg_test_schema fixture should isolate these writes to test_instance_<ts> schemas, but something is causing data to reach the real schema. The 'ticket description scope affected files' rows accumulated confidence 0.78 and caused a 20-message lever_watcher flood on 2026-05-14.

Likely cause: pg_test_schema fixture setup fails silently in some test runs (e.g. IGOR_HOME_DB_URL not set), yielding None. Tests with `if pg_test_schema is None: pytest.skip()` skip cleanly, but any test run where the schema was set up but teardown failed would leave data. Need to audit.

Fix: confirm root cause, then add metadata->>'test_data'='true' marker to all test-inserted rows so they can be cleaned in teardown regardless of schema isolation. Alternatively, add a real-DB check in wp fixture.

**Affected files:** tests/test_watch_problems.py, tests/conftest.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite, test-plan-or-why-not
**Scope boundary:** test isolation only; no production code changes
**Test plan:** verify after fix that running the test suite 3x in a row leaves no rows in instance.watch_problems
